title: Qlog Built-In Sink Adapter Verification
scope: Verify built-in qlog file and stream sink adapters, including frozen snapshot consumption, both JSON qlog output formats, contained CBOR output through the sibling package, deterministic contained-envelope metadata behavior during aggregation, bounded completion behavior, and isolation from other sinks when one sink slows or fails.
verification_method: Specification conformance checks, requirement-home coverage audit, targeted unit tests, and bounded benchmark verification for sink overhead.
preconditions: The qlog SpecTrace artifacts validate successfully under the repository's JSON validation script.
The built-in sink adapter implementation exists.
Test doubles or temporary file/stream destinations are available to observe ordering, format selection, completion, and failure isolation.
procedure: Run scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 over the qlog canonical artifacts.
Run sink adapter unit tests covering contained and sequential JSON file output, contained and sequential JSON stream output, and contained CBOR file and stream output.

Run negative and edge tests for invalid destinations, closed streams, partial-failure cases, contained multi-session aggregation, conflicting shared-envelope metadata with backfill, and non-seekable contained-stream rejection across the implemented sink surfaces.
Run the bounded qlog benchmark dry run if sink work changes the capture hot path.
expected_result: The built-in file and stream sinks consume frozen snapshots, support both JSON qlog formats plus contained CBOR output through the sibling package, preserve deterministic first-writer-wins shared-envelope metadata while backfilling missing values during contained aggregation, complete accepted work predictably, and isolate sink-specific failures without collapsing the capture pipeline back onto transport callbacks or promising more ordering behavior than the internal capture implementation currently owns.
